The '''Rokak'k Baran''' was an immense, ancient [[Starship/Legends|starship]] that was the product of [[Gree technology]]. Disk-shaped and measuring five [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] in diameter, the vessel was the only means of travel between the [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] of the [[Gree Enclave/Legends|Gree Enclave]], a small region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] governed by the [[Gree (species)/Legends|Gree]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of six-tentacled [[Cephalopod/Legends|cephalopods]]. Requiring a crew of 5,000 Gree to operate, the vessel relied on other Gree devices—such as the [[Varat'k Snarap]] and [[Karkak'k Marek]]—for defense.

==Description==
The Rokak'k Baran was a unique example of [[Gree technology]]; a disk-shaped [[Starship/Legends|starship]] with a diameter of five [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]]. Although the vessel possessed no traditional weapons, defensive systems, or [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]], the ship did have [[Gree (species)/Legends|Gree]] [[Master (Gree)|masters]] on board with access to devices that approximated these systems. Among them was the [[Varat'k Snarap]], a device that generated wind-like energy bolts, and the [[Karkak'k Marek]], which functioned as a sensor. The Baran possessed a Class Three [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] for interstellar travel, and was able to carry 250,000 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] of cargo. Enough consumables to last six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] could be stored on board.<ref name="TGE" />

Commanded by [[Rokak'k]], the Baran was the permanent home of several masters and 5,000 Gree—a [[Species/Legends|species]] of six-tentacled [[Cephalopod/Legends|cephalopods]]—who crewed the vessel. 10,000 passengers could be accommodated aboard the Baran, and the fee to travel on the vessel was a small amount for Gree, and in the region of fifteen hundred [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] per round trip for [[Alien/Legends|aliens]]. Cargo could also be carried aboard at an extra cost. Passenger launches were used to ferry [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] and cargo to and from the Baran.<ref name="TGE" />

==History==
Considered to be ancient by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Baran was commanded by the Gree Rokak'k, and was used as a means of transportation between the various [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] of the [[Gree Enclave/Legends|Gree Enclave]], a small region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]]<ref name="TGE" /> in the [[Veragi sector/Legends|Veragi sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA-OC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> As independent travel within the Enclave was discouraged by the Gree, visitors to the Enclave were required to leave their ships on [[Asation]], and book passage on the Baran for transport to other planets. The ship operated a regular circuit of the worlds of the Enclave, stopping at Asation, [[Lonatro]], [[Gree/Legends|Gree]], [[Malanose]], and [[Te Hasa/Legends|Te Hasa]] in turn. The Baran would spend two [[Standard day/Legends|days]] at each planet, then spend a further two days to reach the next destination. The vessel also made one stop at [[Licha In]] per year. As the only means of travel within the Enclave, the Baran was the Gree's only way of transporting messages, news, and goods.<ref name="TGE" />

In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> just prior to the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]] and the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="aotc">{{Film|II}}</ref> [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], leader of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], was spotted aboard the Baran while on a trip to the Enclave to negotiate acquiring a [[Solar sailer/Legends|solar sail]] for his heavily modified [[Punworcca 116-class interstellar sloop/Legends|''Punworcca 116''-class interstellar sloop]].<ref name="HNN">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|54|news/1352_1.html|Dooku Spotted in Gree Enclave}}</ref> Several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later,<ref name="Conjecture">The article states that this event occurred "fifteen years ago"; however, it is unclear which year within the timeline this entry is reporting from. The in-universe articles from the Adventure Journals typically spanned the years between ''A New Hope'' and ''The Empire Strikes Back'', giving a possible date of between 0 ABY—3 ABY, resulting in a possible date of [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]]–[[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]] for this event. However, since no date is firmly established, this article is placing it several years after the known event that occurred in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> the Baran was attacked by a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] as it made its approach to Asation. The pirate ship lightly damaged the vessel before Master [[Varat'k]] could operate the Varat'k Snarap. The Snarap vaporized the frigate with a single energy sweep.<ref name="TGE" />

==Commanders and crew==
The Baran was commanded by Rokak'k, a Gree master who—due to his position in charge of the vessel—was regarded as one of the most influential masters in Gree society during his time. Rokak'k personally operated many of the devices aboard the Baran, and had little time for the aliens that traveled aboard his ship. Rokak'k commanded 5,000 Gree, including lesser masters such as Master Varat'k, who operated the Varat'k Snarap.<ref name="TGE" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Rokak'k Baran was first mentioned in ''[[The Gree Enclave]]'' by [[Timothy S. O'Brien]], an article in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 8|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 8]]. The information was subsequently reprinted in ''[[Classic Adventures: Volume Four — The Best of the Journal]]''. The Rokak'k Baran was later referenced in ''[[HoloNet News Vol. 531 54]]'', an in-universe news article that was released in advance of [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']] to both generate publicity for the film and provide background stories tying the release into the wider [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]].
